enrichment.short_description: Open-source HRMS with attendance, payroll, recruitment, and performance modules, free to self-host or available as paid cloud hosting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Horilla is an open-source HRMS covering attendance, payroll, recruitment, and performance, self-hostable free under LGPL-2.1 or hosted from $7/user/month.

features.title: Employee database
features.description: Central employee records shared across all HR modules.

features.title: Attendance tracking
features.description: Attendance capture with mobile check-in support.

features.title: Payroll
features.description: Built-in payroll processing integrated with attendance and leave data.

features.title: Leave management
features.description: Leave request, approval, and balance tracking.

features.title: Recruitment & onboarding
features.description: Applicant tracking and structured onboarding workflows.

features.title: Performance management
features.description: Employee performance review tracking.

features.title: Asset tracking
features.description: Tracks company assets assigned to employees.

features.title: Helpdesk
features.description: Internal HR ticketing/helpdesk module.

pros: Self-hosted edition is free forever and released under LGPL-2.1 with source on GitHub
All nine HR modules are bundled together rather than sold as separate add-ons
Docker one-click installation simplifies self-hosted deployment
Active open-source community with 820+ forks and 11,000+ deployed teams
Cloud plan includes a 30-day free trial before billing starts
cons: Cloud hosting is billed per user ($7/user/month), which scales with team size
Self-hosting requires the team to manage its own server infrastructure and updates
Enterprise plan pricing is custom/quote-only
Mobile app support is limited primarily to attendance check-in functionality

faqs.question: Is Horilla free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted, open-source edition is free forever under the LGPL-2.1 license.

faqs.question: Does Horilla offer hosted cloud plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, Horilla Cloud is priced at $7 per user per month with a 30-day free trial, plus a custom-priced Enterprise tier with dedicated support.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Horilla?
faqs.answer: Yes, Horilla supports Docker one-click installation as well as manual bare-metal deployment.

faqs.question: Who builds Horilla?
faqs.answer: Horilla is built and maintained by Cybrosys Technologies, headquartered in Kerala, India.

use_cases.title: Unified HR system without per-module fees
use_cases.description: Growing companies that want attendance, payroll, recruitment, and performance tracking in one open platform instead of stitching together separate tools.

use_cases.title: Full data ownership via self-hosting
use_cases.description: Organizations with data residency or privacy requirements that need to keep HR data on their own infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Managed HR without server upkeep
use_cases.description: Teams that want Horilla's feature set without operating their own servers can use the paid cloud-hosted plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Horilla?
paragraphs: Horilla is an open-source Human Resource Management System that bundles attendance, payroll, leave management, recruitment, onboarding, performance management, asset tracking, an employee database, and helpdesk ticketing into a single platform. It's built and maintained by Cybrosys Technologies, based in Kerala, India.

The software is released under the LGPL-2.1 license, and the full source code is available on GitHub, so teams can self-host it for free, audit the code, or fork it. Cybrosys also offers a managed Horilla Cloud option and a custom-priced Enterprise tier for teams that don't want to run their own infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Horilla is for
paragraphs: Horilla suits growing organizations that want a full HR suite without paying separately for each module, especially teams comfortable self-hosting via Docker. It also fits companies that need to keep HR data on infrastructure they control, or that prefer a paid cloud-hosted option ($7/user/month) if they don't want to manage servers themselves.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source Human Capital Management system for recruitment, employee records, performance, and leave management, self-hosted under AGPLv3.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: MintHCM is a free, open-source HCM platform for recruitment, employee profiles, performance, and leave management, self-hosted under AGPLv3.

features.title: Recruitment
features.description: Job descriptions, employer branding, and applicant handling.

features.title: Onboarding & offboarding
features.description: Structured processes for bringing employees in and out.

features.title: Employee profiles & competencies
features.description: Centralized profiles tracking employment history and competencies.

features.title: Performance evaluation
features.description: Tools for tracking and evaluating employee performance.

features.title: Time & leave management
features.description: Time management, calendar, and leave request tracking.

features.title: Travel & expense management
features.description: Tracking of travel and expense requests.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: Reporting on HR processes across the platform.

features.title: Open API
features.description: API access enabling custom integrations with systems like SAP and Workday.

pros: Completely free and open source with no license fees, only self-hosting/server costs
Released under AGPLv3 with source code publicly available on GitHub
Open API allows custom integration with enterprise systems
Community-driven translation into multiple languages via Crowdin
Marketed with AI-enabled features for HR workflows
cons: Requires self-hosting and managing your own server infrastructure
Smaller open-source community (roughly 378 GitHub stars at time of review)
Support is primarily community-forum based, with paid implementation partners as an option
Built on the SuiteCRM/SugarCRM codebase, which may carry some legacy architecture

faqs.question: Is MintHCM free?
faqs.answer: Yes, MintHCM is free and open source; the only ongoing cost is your own server/hosting.

faqs.question: What license is MintHCM released under?
faqs.answer: MintHCM is released under the GNU Affero General Public License v3 (AGPLv3).

faqs.question: Can MintHCM integrate with systems like SAP or Workday?
faqs.answer: MintHCM exposes an open API that can be used for custom integrations with enterprise systems such as SAP and Workday.

faqs.question: Is MintHCM available in languages other than English?
faqs.answer: The interface can be translated into additional languages through MintHCM's community Crowdin project.

use_cases.title: Free self-hosted HCM for cost-conscious teams
use_cases.description: Organizations that want a full HCM feature set without software license fees and are willing to self-host.

use_cases.title: Custom enterprise integration
use_cases.description: Companies needing to connect HR data with existing enterprise systems via the open API.
use_cases.audience: IT teams integrating HR with SAP/Workday

long_form_blocks.heading: What is MintHCM?
paragraphs: MintHCM is a free, open-source Human Capital Management system covering recruitment, onboarding, employee profiles, performance evaluation, time and leave management, and travel/expense tracking. It is released under the AGPLv3 license and self-hosted on the organization's own infrastructure.
The project exposes an open API for integrating with enterprise systems such as SAP and Workday, and its interface can be translated into additional languages through a community Crowdin project.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who MintHCM is for
paragraphs: MintHCM fits organizations that want a no-license-fee HCM system and are comfortable self-hosting and maintaining their own servers, including teams that need to integrate HR data with existing enterprise software via its open API.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source HR management software built on Odoo, covering recruitment, payroll, attendance, and appraisals, with paid support packages available.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Open HRMS is a free, open-source HR management platform built on Odoo, covering recruitment, payroll, attendance, and appraisals.

features.title: Recruitment
features.description: Applicant tracking and recruiting workflow.

features.title: Employee database
features.description: Centralized employee information management.

features.title: Payroll
features.description: Salary and payment processing.

features.title: Appraisal
features.description: Performance management and employee evaluation.

features.title: Attendance & leaves
features.description: Time and leave tracking.

features.title: HR reports
features.description: Analytics and reporting dashboards.

features.title: Timesheets
features.description: Timesheet tracking tied to HR records.

pros: Free, open-source download with no software license fee
Built on the mature Odoo ERP platform, inheriting its module ecosystem
Multiple Odoo version releases supported with installation guides
Cloud demo instance available to evaluate before installing
Paid monthly support packages available for teams that want hands-on help
cons: Self-hosted deployment requires manual installation and PostgreSQL setup
Paid support is incident-based ($499-$1,099/month) rather than a standard SaaS subscription
No hosted/managed cloud pricing is published on the site
Relies on Odoo framework knowledge for deeper customization

faqs.question: Is Open HRMS free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Open HRMS is a free, open-source download; the company also sells optional paid support packages.

faqs.question: What are the paid support packages?
faqs.answer: Open HRMS offers three incident-based monthly support packages priced at $499, $799, and $1,099, covering 15, 30, and 45 support incidents respectively with 24x7 availability.

faqs.question: What platform is Open HRMS built on?
faqs.answer: Open HRMS is built on Odoo, the open-source ERP platform, with HR-specific modules and customizations added.

faqs.question: Who develops Open HRMS?
faqs.answer: Open HRMS is developed by Cybrosys Technologies, with offices in Kerala, India and London, UK.

use_cases.title: Free self-hosted HR on Odoo
use_cases.description: Teams already using or open to Odoo who want a free HR module suite added on top.
use_cases.audience: Odoo users and SMB HR teams

use_cases.title: Supported deployments
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting hands-on setup help and ongoing support can purchase an incident-based support package.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Open HRMS?
paragraphs: Open HRMS is a free, open-source HR management platform built on the Odoo ERP framework. It covers recruitment, employee records, payroll, appraisals, attendance, leave tracking, and HR reporting.
It's developed by Cybrosys Technologies, which also sells optional incident-based support packages ($499-$1,099/month) for teams that want hands-on implementation help alongside the free software.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Open HRMS is for
paragraphs: Open HRMS suits organizations already familiar with Odoo, or those wanting a free HR system they can self-host and customize, with the option to purchase support if they lack in-house Odoo expertise.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source time tracking software for project-driven teams, free to self-host under AGPL-3 or available as GDPR-compliant hosted cloud plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kimai is an open-source time tracking tool for project teams, free to self-host under AGPL-3 or hosted from €2.99/user/month with EU-based cloud plans.

features.title: Time tracking & reporting
features.description: Track and analyze company time data with built-in reporting.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Configurable invoice templates in PDF and DOCX, including e-invoicing (EN 16931, XRechnung 3.0).

features.title: JSON API
features.description: API for external integrations.

features.title: SSO authentication
features.description: LDAP and SAML login support, including Google Workspace, Azure AD, and Authentik.

features.title: Two-factor authentication
features.description: TOTP-based 2FA for account security.

features.title: Customer & project management
features.description: Manage customers and projects with role-based permissions.

features.title: Data export
features.description: Export time data as XLSX, PDF, CSV, or HTML.

features.title: Customer portal
features.description: Share statistics with customers via a dedicated portal.

pros: Self-hosted edition is free forever and 100% open source under AGPL-3
Cloud plans include GDPR-compliant hosting in Germany with automatic updates and backups
Translated into 30+ languages
Built-in e-invoicing compliant with EN 16931 and XRechnung 3.0 standards
Supports enterprise-grade SSO via SAML and LDAP
cons: No native mobile apps are mentioned; access is web-based
Higher-tier features (e.g., SSO, customer portal, custom domain) require the paid Professional cloud plan
Self-hosting requires managing your own server and updates

faqs.question: Is Kimai free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted version is free forever and fully open source under the AGPL-3 license.

faqs.question: What does Kimai Cloud cost?
faqs.answer: Kimai Cloud starts at €2.99/user/month (Standard, billed annually) or €3.99/user/month for the Professional plan billed annually, with 20% savings versus monthly billing.

faqs.question: Where is Kimai Cloud hosted?
faqs.answer: Kimai Cloud is hosted in Germany and is GDPR compliant.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Kimai?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kimai supports Docker and manual self-hosted installation.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted time tracking for full data control
use_cases.description: Teams that want to run time tracking on their own infrastructure at no license cost.

use_cases.title: Managed EU-hosted time tracking
use_cases.description: Teams that want Kimai's features without server maintenance can use the GDPR-compliant cloud plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Kimai?
paragraphs: Kimai is an open-source time tracking platform for project-driven teams, offering time tracking, reporting, invoicing, and a JSON API. The self-hosted edition is free and released under the AGPL-3 license.

For teams that don't want to run their own server, Kimai also offers hosted Cloud plans (Standard and Professional) with GDPR-compliant hosting in Germany, starting at €2.99/user/month billed annually.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Kimai is for
paragraphs: Kimai suits project-based teams, agencies, and consultancies that need time tracking with invoicing, whether they prefer to self-host for free or use a managed EU-hosted cloud plan with SSO and customer-facing reporting.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source, privacy-first automatic time tracker that stores all activity data locally on the user's device.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ActivityWatch is a free, open-source automatic time tracker that logs app and website usage locally, with no premium tiers or data sent to servers.

features.title: Automatic activity tracking
features.description: Tracks active applications and window titles without manual timers.

features.title: Local-first storage
features.description: All time tracking data stays on the user's own device and is never sent to a server.

features.title: Browser extensions
features.description: Chrome and Firefox extensions capture web browsing activity.

features.title: Editor plugins
features.description: Plugins for tracking coding time inside editors.

features.title: Categorization
features.description: Tools for organizing tracked activity into categories.

features.title: Custom watchers
features.description: Extendable watcher system for capturing additional activity types.

pros: Completely free with no premium tiers, usage limits, or ads
Open source under the MPL-2.0 license
Privacy-first: all data stored locally on-device rather than sent to a server
Cross-platform support for Windows, macOS, Linux, and Android
Extensible via browser extensions, editor plugins, and custom watchers
cons: Cross-device synchronization is still in development
No built-in cloud dashboard or team/manager reporting, since data stays local
Developed by volunteers, so support relies on the open-source community

faqs.question: Is ActivityWatch free?
faqs.answer: Yes, ActivityWatch is completely free with no premium tiers, usage limits, or ads.

faqs.question: Where is my tracked data stored?
faqs.answer: All time tracking data is stored locally on your own device and is never sent to any server.

faqs.question: What platforms does ActivityWatch support?
faqs.answer: ActivityWatch supports Windows, macOS, Linux, and Android.

faqs.question: What license is ActivityWatch released under?
faqs.answer: ActivityWatch is licensed under the Mozilla Public License 2.0 (MPL-2.0).

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious personal time tracking
use_cases.description: Individuals who want automatic activity tracking without sending data to a third-party server.

use_cases.title: Developer time tracking
use_cases.description: Developers who want to track coding time via editor plugins alongside general app/website usage.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ActivityWatch?
paragraphs: ActivityWatch is a free, open-source automatic time tracker that logs active applications, window titles, and web browsing activity to give users insight into how they spend time on their devices.

It follows a local-first, privacy-focused model: all tracked data is stored on the user's own device and never transmitted to a server. The project is licensed under MPL-2.0 and supports Windows, macOS, Linux, and Android, with browser extensions and editor plugins extending what it can track.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ActivityWatch is for
paragraphs: ActivityWatch fits individuals and developers who want automated personal time tracking without any subscription cost or cloud data collection, including those who specifically want their activity data to never leave their device.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source, self-hosted, tag-based time tracking tool with dashboards, calendar views, and a Docker deployment.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Traggo is a free, open-source, self-hosted, tag-based time tracking tool with customizable dashboards and calendar views.

features.title: Tag-based tracking
features.description: Organize tracked time using flexible custom tags instead of fixed tasks or projects.

features.title: Customizable dashboards
features.description: Build dashboards with diagrams from tracked time data.

features.title: List and calendar views
features.description: View tracked time entries as a list or on a calendar.

features.title: Theming
features.description: Web UI with multiple theme options.

features.title: User management
features.description: Manage multiple users on a self-hosted instance.

pros: Completely free and open source with no commercial pricing tier
Full data control since self-hosting is the only deployment option
Flexible tag-based organization instead of rigid project/task structures
Docker-based deployment for straightforward setup
Customizable dashboards and both list and calendar views
cons: No hosted/cloud version is offered; self-hosting is required
Built by a small open-source project, so support relies on the community
Tag-based model may require more manual setup than task/project-based tools

faqs.question: Is Traggo free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Traggo is completely free and open source.

faqs.question: Does Traggo offer a hosted/cloud version?
faqs.answer: No, Traggo is self-hosted only; there is no cloud-hosted option.

faqs.question: How does Traggo organize tracked time?
faqs.answer: Traggo uses a tag-based system, letting users categorize time entries with custom tags instead of predefined projects or tasks.

faqs.question: How is Traggo deployed?
faqs.answer: Traggo is deployed via Docker for self-hosting.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted personal/team time tracking
use_cases.description: Users who want a free, self-hosted time tracker with full control over their data.

use_cases.title: Flexible tag-based categorization
use_cases.description: Users who prefer organizing tracked time by custom tags rather than a fixed project/task hierarchy.
use_cases.audience: Users with non-standard time categorization needs

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Traggo?
paragraphs: Traggo is a free, open-source, self-hosted time tracking tool that organizes tracked time using custom tags rather than traditional tasks or projects. It offers customizable dashboards, list and calendar views, and a themeable web UI.
Traggo is deployed via Docker and is self-hosted only, meaning there is no cloud-hosted version; all tracked data stays on infrastructure the user controls.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Traggo is for
paragraphs: Traggo suits individuals and small teams who want a free, self-hosted time tracker with a flexible, tag-based way of categorizing time rather than a rigid project/task structure.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source time tracking app for teams and freelancers, with a free solo plan, paid Professional cloud plan, and self-hosting option.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Solidtime is an open-source time tracker with a free solo plan, invoicing, desktop apps, and paid Professional cloud plans from €8/user/month.

features.title: Timer, calendar & weekly views
features.description: Track time via a timer, calendar, or weekly timesheet view.

features.title: Reporting
features.description: Filter, share, and export reports of tracked hours.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Convert billable hours into invoices, with VAT and discount support.

features.title: Multi-project support
features.description: Manage multiple projects with rates, team roles, and access controls.

features.title: Desktop app
features.description: Automatic activity tracking and idle detection with local-only privacy storage.

features.title: Browser extension
features.description: Browser extension for tracking time alongside the desktop app.

pros: Free Solo plan available with no credit card required to start
Open source under AGPL-3.0 with over 8,600 GitHub stars
Self-hosting option available for organizations that want on-premise deployment
EU-hosted cloud infrastructure with GDPR-compliant, privacy-first design
Desktop apps for macOS, Windows, and Linux with idle detection
cons: Free plan is limited to a single user
Invoicing, team features, and advanced reporting require the paid Professional plan
No specific founding year or headquarters is published on the site

faqs.question: Is Solidtime free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidtime offers a free Solo plan for a single user with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Solidtime's paid plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Professional plan costs €9/user/month billed monthly, or €8/user/month billed annually.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Solidtime?
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidtime is open source (AGPL-3.0) and offers a self-hosting/on-premise option.

faqs.question: What platforms does Solidtime support?
faqs.answer: Solidtime is available as a web app, desktop apps for macOS, Windows, and Linux, and a browser extension.

use_cases.title: Freelancer time tracking and invoicing
use_cases.description: Freelancers who track billable hours and convert them into invoices.

use_cases.title: Team time tracking with access controls
use_cases.description: Teams needing project rates, role-based access, and shareable reports.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted deployment
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting to run Solidtime on their own infrastructure instead of the cloud version.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Solidtime?
paragraphs: Solidtime is an open-source time tracking application for teams and freelancers, offering a timer, calendar and weekly timesheet views, reporting, invoicing, and multi-project support. It's released under AGPL-3.0 and has over 8,600 GitHub stars.

The product is offered as a free Solo cloud plan for a single user, a paid Professional cloud plan (from €8/user/month annually) for teams needing invoicing and access controls, and a self-hosted on-premise option for organizations that prefer to run it themselves.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Solidtime is for
paragraphs: Solidtime fits freelancers who want free solo time tracking and invoicing, as well as agencies and client-facing teams that need multi-project rates, team access controls, and shareable reports, with self-hosting available for teams that prefer to control their own data.

enrichment.short_description: Time tracking software for service teams to capture billable hours, manage billing rates, and track project profitability.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TMetric is time tracking software for agencies and client-facing teams, offering billable hours, invoicing, and project budgets, free for up to 2 seats.

features.title: Automated time tracking
features.description: Real-time tracking across desktop, browser, and mobile apps.

features.title: Project profitability
features.description: Budget tracking and cost-versus-revenue analysis per project.

features.title: Billing & invoicing
features.description: Automatic invoice generation applying configured billing rates.

features.title: Team management
features.description: Dashboards, work schedules, and team activity tracking.

features.title: Timesheets
features.description: Automated timesheet calculation with approval workflows.

features.title: Reporting
features.description: Reports on projects, tasks, team activity, and app usage.

features.title: Time off management
features.description: Leave requests, balances, and accrual tracking.

features.title: App & site monitoring
features.description: Screenshot capture and activity tracking for teams (Business plan).

pros: Free plan available for up to 2 seats with unlimited time tracking, clients, and projects
14-day free trial with no credit card required, extendable by 7 more days
50+ integrations including Jira, Asana, QuickBooks, GitHub, and Trello
Available across web, desktop (Windows/macOS), mobile (iOS/Android), and browser extensions
4.6-star rating from 3,000+ reviews and used by 10,000+ companies
cons: Exact per-seat pricing for Professional and Business plans is not publicly listed
Advanced features like invoicing, time off, and screenshot monitoring require paid tiers
Enterprise plan requires custom/quote-based pricing

faqs.question: Does TMetric have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, TMetric offers a free plan for up to 2 seats with unlimited time tracking, clients, and projects.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, TMetric offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required, extendable by 7 additional days.

faqs.question: What platforms does TMetric support?
faqs.answer: TMetric is available as a web app, Windows and macOS desktop apps, iOS and Android mobile apps, and browser extensions for Chrome, Firefox, Opera, Edge, and Safari.

faqs.question: What integrations does TMetric offer?
faqs.answer: TMetric integrates with 50+ tools including Jira, Asana, QuickBooks, Notion, GitHub, GitLab, Zendesk, Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, and Trello.

use_cases.title: Billable hours for agencies
use_cases.description: Agencies and consultancies tracking billable hours, billing rates, and project margins.

use_cases.title: Client-facing project profitability
use_cases.description: Client-facing teams needing budget tracking and cost-vs-revenue analysis per project.

use_cases.title: Team activity monitoring
use_cases.description: Teams wanting app/site monitoring and screenshot capture alongside time tracking.

features: Calendar sync with Google and Outlook

long_form_blocks.heading: What is TMetric?
paragraphs: TMetric is time tracking software built for service teams to capture billable hours, manage billing rates, and control project margins. It targets agencies, consultancies, and client-facing teams looking to reduce revenue leakage from untracked time.

It offers a free plan for up to 2 seats, paid Professional and Business plans that add invoicing, time off management, and app/site monitoring, and a custom-priced Enterprise plan, with apps across web, desktop, and mobile.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who TMetric is for
paragraphs: TMetric suits agencies, consultancies, and other client-facing teams that need to track billable hours, manage project budgets, and generate invoices, from small teams using the free tier to larger teams needing team timesheets and activity monitoring.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered time tracking platform with automated timesheets, invoicing, and attendance/payroll reporting, with a free plan for unlimited users.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TimeCamp is an AI-powered time tracker with automated timesheets, invoicing, and payroll reporting, free for unlimited users or from $3.99/user/month.

features.title: AI Time Tracking Agent (TIC)
features.description: Automatically fills timesheets by monitoring applications, websites, and calendar activity.

features.title: Automated & manual tracking
features.description: One-click timer, manual entry, and background activity monitoring.

features.title: Timesheet approvals
features.description: Managers review, approve, or reject entries with audit-ready locking.

features.title: Real-time dashboards
features.description: Productivity metrics, labor costs, and workforce capacity data.

features.title: Invoicing & billing
features.description: Create invoices from billable hours with custom billing rates.

features.title: Attendance & payroll
features.description: Tracks time-off and overtime, generating payroll-ready reports.

features.title: Compliance features
features.description: Supports GDPR, SOC2, HIPAA, and DCAA standards with role-based access.

pros: Free plan supports unlimited users, projects, and timesheets with core tracking
14-day free trial on paid plans with no credit card required
AI Time Tracking Agent automatically fills timesheets from app, website, and calendar activity
50+ integrations including Jira, Asana, Trello, ClickUp, and Monday.com
Available on web, Windows, macOS, and Linux desktop apps
4.7-star rating from 1,500+ reviews and used by 18,000+ teams in 102 countries
cons: AI tracking, invoicing, and attendance features require the paid Starter plan or above
Data export and timesheet approvals are limited to the higher Ultimate plan
Enterprise pricing (private cloud, SSO, custom integrations) is custom/quote-only

faqs.question: Is TimeCamp free?
faqs.answer: Yes, TimeCamp offers a free plan with unlimited users, projects, and timesheets, plus core time tracking.

faqs.question: How much do TimeCamp's paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at Starter ($3.99/user/month billed annually), Premium ($6.99/user/month), and Ultimate ($9.99/user/month), with a custom-priced Enterprise tier above that.

faqs.question: Does TimeCamp offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What is the TimeCamp AI Time Tracking Agent (TIC)?
faqs.answer: TIC is an AI agent that automatically fills in timesheets by monitoring applications, websites, and calendar activity, available on Starter plans and above.

use_cases.title: Automated timesheets
use_cases.description: Teams that want timesheets filled in automatically rather than tracked manually.

use_cases.title: Project profitability & invoicing
use_cases.description: Agencies and consultancies billing clients based on tracked, billable hours.

use_cases.title: Payroll and attendance reporting
use_cases.description: HR and finance teams needing payroll-ready attendance and overtime reports.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is TimeCamp?
paragraphs: TimeCamp is a time tracking platform that combines automated and manual time logging with an AI agent (TIC) that fills in timesheets by monitoring applications, websites, and calendar activity. It also offers timesheet approvals, invoicing, and payroll-ready attendance reporting.

The Free plan supports unlimited users and core tracking, while paid Starter, Premium, and Ultimate plans add AI tracking, invoicing, resource planning, and data export, with a custom-priced Enterprise tier for private cloud and SSO needs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who TimeCamp is for
paragraphs: TimeCamp fits teams that want low-effort, largely automated time tracking, as well as agencies needing project billing and invoicing, and HR/finance teams needing payroll and attendance reports.

enrichment.short_description: Automatic time tracking and productivity monitoring software for remote, hybrid, and on-site teams, founded in 2011 by Latvia's Draugiem Group.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: DeskTime is automatic time tracking and productivity monitoring software for remote and hybrid teams, from €5.50/user/month with a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Automatic time tracking
features.description: Background activity monitoring without manual entry.

features.title: Productivity analysis
features.description: Calculates productivity metrics and identifies focus patterns.

features.title: Activity monitoring
features.description: URL/app tracking, document title monitoring, and screenshot capture.

features.title: Workforce management
features.description: Shift scheduling, absence calendars, and attendance tracking.

features.title: Reporting & analytics
features.description: Customizable reports with admin and user dashboards, including AI-powered summaries.

features.title: Private time
features.description: Lets employees pause tracking during non-work activities.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Jira, Asana, Outlook, Google Calendar, GitLab, Trello, Zapier, and via API.

pros: Founded in 2011 as part of Latvia's Draugiem Group, with 730,000+ users and 330 million+ hours tracked
14-day free trial across plans, plus a bonus free month with annual billing
ISO 27001 & 27701 certified with GDPR compliance and two-factor authentication
4.5/5 rating on G2 from 400+ reviews
Screenshot capture and shift scheduling included on higher tiers
cons: No free plan; only a 14-day free trial
Screenshots, integrations, and API access require the Premium plan or above
Enterprise plan requires a minimum of 200+ users
Prices are listed in EUR/USD and exclude VAT

faqs.question: Does DeskTime have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, DeskTime does not offer a permanent free plan, but it does offer a 14-day free trial and a free month when billed annually.

faqs.question: How much does DeskTime cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan starts at €5.50/user/month, and Premium is €8.25/user/month, both billed annually or monthly; Enterprise (200+ users) is custom priced.

faqs.question: When was DeskTime founded?
faqs.answer: DeskTime was created in 2011 as an internal tool within Draugiem Group, a Latvia-based business incubator, and remains part of that company.

faqs.question: Is DeskTime GDPR compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, DeskTime is ISO 27001 & 27701 certified and GDPR compliant, with data encryption and two-factor authentication support.

use_cases.title: Remote/hybrid team productivity monitoring
use_cases.description: Managers tracking productivity and attendance across distributed teams.
use_cases.audience: Managers of remote and hybrid teams

use_cases.title: Shift-based workforce management
use_cases.description: Organizations needing shift scheduling and absence calendars alongside time tracking.

use_cases.title: Large enterprise deployments
use_cases.description: Large organizations (200+ users) needing custom API integrations and a dedicated account manager.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is DeskTime?
paragraphs: DeskTime is automatic time tracking and productivity monitoring software for remote, hybrid, and on-site teams. It captures work activity in the background, including app/URL usage, document titles, and optional screenshots, and turns it into productivity reports, shift schedules, and attendance data.
DeskTime was created in 2011 within Draugiem Group, a Latvia-based business incubator headquartered in Riga, and remains part of that company today. It's ISO 27001/27701 certified and GDPR compliant.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who DeskTime is for
paragraphs: DeskTime suits managers of remote and hybrid teams who need automatic productivity monitoring, as well as shift-based and on-site teams needing scheduling and attendance tracking, up to large enterprises needing custom integrations.
